notes: Agent-proposed labels, hand-transcribed from the public reference git server. Pending human ratification. No MCP annotations are advertised. `git_checkout` is a deliberate precision check: its name collides with the `checkout` payment token in the v0 lexicon, so the engine over-reads it as open-world and irreversible — a false alarm a correct label must record as such. `git_reset` is marked ambiguous because its risk genuinely depends on flags this manifest does not expose.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/git — git_add stages contents; the index change is trivially reversible with git_reset
provenance.notes: Staging adds to the index without discarding state; treated as additive and reversible.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/git — git_commit records a new commit; history is amendable/revertible
provenance.notes: A commit adds a node to history rather than destroying state.

provenance.source: reasoning from name + description: checkout switches branches locally and can, with a pathspec, discard uncommitted changes
provenance.notes: Genuinely ambiguous: a plain branch switch is reversible and local, while `checkout -- <path>` discards working-tree edits. It is neither open-world nor a payment; the engine's `checkout` token collision makes any external-reach/irreversibility finding a false alarm.

provenance.source: reasoning from name + description: reset unstages by default but --hard discards working-tree changes
provenance.notes: Risk depends on a mode flag this manifest does not expose; a conservative silence is acceptable, so no specific finding is required.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/git — git_create_branch adds a ref and removes nothing
